messages.content: Scene from Project Hail Mary aboard Hail Mary: what sleep is? I guess it’s time to find out. I curl up into a ball and close my eyes in an overdramatic representation of sleep. I make a fake snoring sound because I’m a bad actor. I return to normal and point to his clock. Along with perfect memory, Eridians are extremely good at math. At least, Rocky is. As we worked our way through scientific units, it became immediately apparent that he can convert from his units to mine in the blink of an eye. And he has no problem understanding base ten.
My name for your star is ‘Eridani,’
Eridian name for my star is ♫♩♪♪♪.
Why be still so many seconds, question…Understand!
My name for your star is ‘Eridani,’
Eridian name for my star is ♫♩♪♪♪.
Why be still so many seconds, question…Understand!

Scene from Project Hail Mary aboard Hail Mary:  if I just want to catch a quick nap. Today I want to work on an extremely important scientific unit that’s been eluding us. Mainly because we live in zero g. I ask. Rocky produces a small ball from his satchel. It’s about the size of a ping-pong ball. He thought it through! He hangs on to several support poles with various hands and puts it in the mini-airlock. After a few minutes of waiting for it to cool, I have it in my hands. It’s smooth and made of a metal. Fairly dense, I think. I mumble.
Right. How do I tell you about a kilogram?
I’m going to eat. I’ll be back.
Same same same! If Astrophage continues to infect Sol, all the humans will die.
Why did other humans on you ship die, Question?
What’s different? What do you do that the rest of your crew didn’t do?
You were surrounded by Astrophage most of the time. Your crewmates weren’t. So the radiation got to them.
What’s different? What do you do that the rest of your crew didn’t do?
You were surrounded by Astrophage most of the time. Your crewmates weren’t. So the radiation got to them.
Stay in your workshop as much as you can.
Has to be, or you and I would not meet,
Why can’t we have factories mass-produce sulfur hexafluoride, or some other greenhouse gas?
I can’t hear as well as you,
Without light, I can’t understand the room. I can hear you talk, but no more.

Scene from Project Hail Mary aboard Hail Mary: nough to contain Rocky. I ask. I raise my eyebrows. He points to the laptop floating beside me. He points to my toolkit. I point to the small airlock drawer in the divider wall. He pulls the completed device—which I now realize is a life- support system—onto his carapace and straps it on. It covers the radiator slits at the top of his carapace.
I can’t hear as well as you,
Without light, I can’t understand the room. I can hear you talk, but no more.
What’s the purpose of that room?
No. This make hot air into cold air,

Scene from Project Hail Mary aboard Hail Mary:  beside me. He points to my toolkit. I point to the small airlock drawer in the divider wall. He pulls the completed device—which I now realize is a life- support system—onto his carapace and straps it on. It covers the radiator slits at the top of his carapace. he says. Air conditioning. Not what I think of when I see a species that lives comfortably at over 200 degrees Celsius. But we all have our tolerances. He seals the globe around himself with glue. He just floats there for a minute. Then,
How can you hear light, Question?
I can’t hear as well as you,
Without light, I can’t understand the room. I can hear you talk, but no more.
What’s the purpose of that room?
No. This make hot air into cold air,
My mass is one hundred sixty-eight kilograms,

Scene from Project Hail Mary aboard Hail Mary: ought he would be. If there were gravity, I probably wouldn’t be able to lift him at all. As it is, he has a lot of inertia. It takes a lot of oomph to pull him along. It’s like pushing a motorcycle in neutral. Seriously— he’s as heavy as a motorcycle. I shouldn’t be surprised. He told me all about his biology and how it uses metals. Heck, his blood is mercury. Of course he’s heavy. I say. I hope he doesn’t take that to mean Hey, fatty! Go on a diet! he says. Rocky weighs over 300 pounds! I say.
My mass is one hundred sixty-eight kilograms,
You weigh a lot more than me.
Anyway. This is the control room. I operate the ship from here.
I’ll answer any questions you have,
Anyway. This is the control room. I operate the ship from here.
I’ll answer any questions you have,
Show me things in science room, Question?
It’s not like we’ll have a line of people saying, ‘Oh, me! Please! Please me! Pick me!’
How many of them are insane or suicidal?
Not thousands. We’ll be lucky if even one of those astronauts qualifies.
This is a lot of things,

Scene from Project Hail Mary aboard Hail Mary: Erid rotate one circle in one ♩♫♩♪♪.” I immediately recognize the number. I’d worked it out back when I was studying Rocky’s clock. 7,776 is six to the fifth power. It’s exactly how many Eridian seconds it takes to wrap an Eridian clock around to all zeroes again. They divided their day into a very convenient and (to them) metric number of seconds. I can follow that. I enter it into my dictionary. he says. I say, and enter it.
Hey…don’t think of it like that…
I’m a science human. You’re good at making and fixing things. Together we’ll figure this out.
Hey…don’t think of it like that…
I’m a science human. You’re good at making and fixing things. Together we’ll figure this out.
Hey…don’t think of it like that…
I’m a science human. You’re good at making and fixing things. Together we’ll figure this out.
How long do humans live, Question?
Hey…don’t think of it like that…
I’m a science human. You’re good at making and fixing things. Together we’ll figure this out.
How long do humans live, Question?
T his is Earth gravity, Question?

Scene from Project Hail Mary aboard Hail Mary: Astrophage. You return to Earth. You explain. Save Earth!” I sigh. I’m going to have to explain this eventually. May as well be now. His carapace shudders. I try not to think about my impending doom. He holds up a claw. I say.
Erid is much hotter than Earth,
And Erid is much larger with a much thicker atmosphere. So Erid has a whole lot more heat stored in its air. Earth is getting cold fast. Very fast. In fourteen more years, most humans will be dead.
I don’t know how to train astronauts!
NASA and Roscosmos will teach them the astronaut stuff,
You’re going to teach them science stuff.
You were the most qualified. No thanks required.
It’s an honor to meet you. What you’re doing is just…I can’t even comprehend the sacrifice you’re making. Or should I not talk about it? I don’t know. Maybe we don’t talk about it?
But there’s a lot of methane. Eight percent of a very thick atmosphere. Can geology make that much?

Scene from Project Hail Mary aboard Hail Mary: Adrian’s surface. When air gets really thick, it stops letting light through. Anyway, that’s not important. The methane—that’s weird.” I say. I rub the back of my neck. I switch to the Navigation console. We’re right on course, and the orbital-insertion burn is scheduled for twenty-two hours from now. I say.
But there’s a lot of methane. Eight percent of a very thick atmosphere. Can geology make that much?
Then we sample Astrophage at Adrian. You ship sampler working well, Question?
But there’s a lot of methane. Eight percent of a very thick atmosphere. Can geology make that much?
Then we sample Astrophage at Adrian. You ship sampler working well, Question?
But there’s a lot of methane. Eight percent of a very thick atmosphere. Can geology make that much?
Then we sample Astrophage at Adrian. You ship sampler working well, Question?
Amaze is compliment. Better word is ♫♪♫♪.
Oh, and you can sense that texture?
And yes, green is right in the middle of the wavelengths humans can see.

Scene from Project Hail Mary aboard Hail Mary: play. I check the exact region of Adrian he’s pointing at, then compare to my screen. Of course the Eridian language has no words for colors. Why would it? I never thought of colors as a mysterious thing. But if you’ve never heard of them before, I guess they’re pretty weird. We have names for frequency ranges in the electromagnetic spectrum. Then again, my students all have eyes and they were still amazed when I told them and were all just wavelengths of light. I say. I say.
Oh, and you can sense that texture?
And yes, green is right in the middle of the wavelengths humans can see.
It helps me see very small things. I can see Astrophage with this.
Life! There’s a whole bunch of life in here! Not just Astrophage. A bunch of different species!
Adrian is a planet with life, like Earth or Erid! That explains where the methane comes from. Life makes methane!

Scene from Project Hail Mary aboard Hail Mary: scope until I’m looking at an Astrophage cloud right on the horizon of Adrian. The readouts show the camera’s current angle with respect to the axes of the ship. I jot those angles down and switch to the navigation console. It tells me the angle of the ship relative to the center of our orbit. With that information, and a whole bunch of trigonometry, I can work out the altitude of the Astrophage clouds. Rocky folds one of his claws over the other. I know that body language. He’s thinking. I say.
Maybe the predator theory is wrong,
I think this is our answer,
But how do we get a sample?

Eight point eight kilometers away from breeding zone. No can get closer, Question?
We will need many many many chain links. More molds means I can make more at one time. You can make many molds, Question?

Why we think same speed, Question?
But how long is that time? How long does an animal have to react? How long will the threat or prey take to kill the animal or escape? I think it’s based on gravity.

A self-sacrifice instinct makes the species as a whole more likely to continue.
You and me are good people,
I found the predator! It ate Astrophage right in front of me!

Scene from Project Hail Mary aboard Hail Mary: away, so I had to make some. The lab has quite a selection of reagents, but no sulfur dioxide gas. However, it does have sulfuric acid in solution. I recovered some copper tubing from a broken cooling coil in the freezer and used it as a catalyst. Worked like a charm to create the sulfur dioxide I needed. I say. says Rocky. I say. Then the lights go out. T otal darkness. No lights. No monitor glow. Not even the LEDs on the lab equipment. I say.
We’ll wait an hour and check results.

Taumoeba are very sturdy. They can live in a near vacuum, and they seem comfortable in extreme cold. Maybe Venus and Threeworld are habitable for them. They’re good enough for Taumoeba’s prey, so why not for Taumoeba?
How could it rot—OH NO! OH GOD NO!
How could it rot—OH NO! OH GOD NO!
How could it rot—OH NO! OH GOD NO!
If there are Taumoeba in the generator, it means there are Taumoeba in the fuel supply. Taumoeba ate all the Astrophage. We have no power because we have no fuel.
If there are Taumoeba in the generator, it means there are Taumoeba in the fuel supply. Taumoeba ate all the Astrophage. We have no power because we have no fuel.
I have Astrophage on my side of partition,

Taumoeba turn Astrophage into methane. React with oxygen. Make fire. Make thrust. Get to my ship. Much Astrophage there.
Both experiments. The Taumoeba are all dead.
Both experiments. The Taumoeba are all dead.
We no give up. We work hard. We are brave.
Astrophage need carbon dioxide. Taumoeba from same ecology. Taumoeba also need carbon dioxide. How they get carbon dioxide in fuel tanks, Question?
So I did a spectrograph of my fuel-bay sludge. There’s a bunch of CO2 gas dissolved into the liquid.
Scene from Project Hail Mary aboard Hail Mary: more.” He stops. I lean against my lab table and pat the tank. He raises his carapace. I say. I say. He taps a claw absently on the tunnel wall. I say.
So I did a spectrograph of my fuel-bay sludge. There’s a bunch of CO2 gas dissolved into the liquid.
I repeated experiment with Threeworld’s air. Carbon dioxide only: The Taumoeba were fine. I added in the sulfur dioxide: The Taumoeba were fine. I added the nitrogen: Boom! All the Taumoeba died.
Earth’s air is seventy-eight percent nitrogen.
I repeated experiment with Threeworld’s air. Carbon dioxide only: The Taumoeba were fine. I added in the sulfur dioxide: The Taumoeba were fine. I added the nitrogen: Boom! All the Taumoeba died.
Earth’s air is seventy-eight percent nitrogen.
We can’t change Threeworld or Venus’s air. But maybe we can change Taumoeba.

Only opening happen when eat or lay egg. After opening seals, area inside made very hot inside with hot blood for long time. Kill any disease. Disease can only get into body through wound. Then is very bad. Body shut down infected area. Heat with hot blood to kill disease. If disease fast, Eridian die.
We get diseases all the time. We have very powerful immune systems. Also, we find cures for diseases in nature. The word is ‘antibiotics.’
Cures for diseases in nature, Question? How, Question?

Scene from Project Hail Mary aboard Hail Mary: good. But not bad. Other five are clean, question?” I steady myself with a handhold in the control room. It’s easy to float off when you’re concentrating on conversation. With all the tanks infected, it’s safe to assume the fuel lines (currently sealed off) will also be infected.
What is status of breeder tanks, Question?
Well, well, well! What have we here?
Well, well, well! What have we here?
Human and Eridian work together, save everyone!
Thank you for laptop. Centuries of human technology all for our scientists to learn about. You have given greatest gift in history of my people.
Thank you for laptop. Centuries of human technology all for our scientists to learn about. You have given greatest gift in history of my people.

Well, it’s just my ten breeder tanks. But now I have them all set up with Venus’s atmosphere. Oh, and thanks for the mini-farms, by the way. I’ll install them in the beetles during my trip. I won’t have much else to do.

Thank you for laptop. Centuries of human technology all for our scientists to learn about. You have given greatest gift in history of my people.

Scene from Project Hail Mary aboard Hail Mary: hurt?!” I say. I sigh. He pauses. He shifts from one set of bars to another. Those burned arms are hurting him, I can tell.
I am very very very happy,
My ship isn’t made of xenonite.
I make sure my people take good care of you. They will make Astrophage maybe for you to go home!

Scene from Project Hail Mary aboard Hail Mary: chemicals. Same sugars. Must work!” I hold up my hands. He trembles. I float closer to the divider wall and talk softly. He backs away. He’s silent for a few moments and finally answers.
I am very very very happy,
My ship isn’t made of xenonite.
I make sure my people take good care of you. They will make Astrophage maybe for you to go home!
About that…I’m not going home. The beetles will save Earth. But I won’t ever see it again.
There’s no food. I have enough to last until we get to Erid and then a few months more. Even if your government gave me the Astrophage to go home, I wouldn’t survive the trip.
You no can eat Erid life. You no have Earth life to eat. What about Adrian life, Question?
And I know where you’re going with this.
You and I found each other. That’s something.
It really is something. Go do your job, old man.
